case.title: DULUTH, S. SHORE & ATL. RR. v. MICHIGAN CORP., SEC.

opinion.h4: DULUTH, S. SHORE & ATL. RR. v. MICHIGAN CORP., SEC., (1959)
DULUTH, SOUTH SHORE & ATLANTIC RAILROAD CO. v. MICHIGAN CORPORATION AND SECURITIES COMMISSION ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF MICHIGAN. No. 712. Decided April 20, 1959.
opinion.p: Henry I. Armstrong, Jr. and Louis F. Dahling for appellant.
Paul L. Adams, Attorney General of Michigan, Samuel J. Torina, Solicitor General, and William D. Dexter, Assistant Attorney General, for appellees.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for certiorari, certiorari is denied.
opinion.h4: WHYTE v. COAST CITIES COACHES, INC., (1959)
opinion.p: WHYTE v. COAST CITIES COACHES, INC., ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF FLORIDA. No. 747. Decided April 20, 1959.
The appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for certiorari, certiorari is denied.
